exemplar.title: To what extent did Amazon's post-Covid-19 diversification strategies impact its revenue growth and profitablity?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did Amazon's post-Covid-19 diversification strategies impact its revenue growth and profitablity?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition of the key concept of change
Consistent linkage of âchangeâ to Amazonâs diversification moves
Relevant Amazon-specific examples (Whole Foods, Alexa) in analysis
weaknesses: Analysis seldom framed by a change-management model
Conceptual integration is occasional rather than sustained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows a good understanding of âchangeâ and repeatedly connects it to Amazonâs strategy, matching the band-3 descriptor for clear analytical thinking. However, it lacks a sustained, systematic application of a formal change-management framework, so it does not fully meet the band-4 standard. A score of 3 reflects this solid but not comprehensive integration.

strengths: Five diverse and relevant sources cited
weaknesses: Depth of analysis uneven across sources
Missing retrieval dates or DOIs for some web references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student provides the required number of documents and they are broadly relevant, the depth is uneven and some references lack full academic rigor (missing dates/DOIs). This aligns with the band-2 descriptor for generally relevant documents that lack consistent depth.

strengths: Correct application of the market-share formula
Use of the Ansoff Matrix to analyze diversification
weaknesses: Linkages between tools and research question are sometimes superficial
Lacks detail on triangulation or validation of conflicting sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student selects appropriate strategic and quantitative tools, but the application is only superficially connected to the research question and methodological details are sparse. This supports a band-2 rating for some tool selection with limited application clarity.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Relevant quantitative data selected (revenues, margins, market share)
Basic trend analysis linking diversification to performance
weaknesses: Insufficient integration of counter-arguments or deeper risk evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is adequate and some trends are analyzed, but the treatment is mostly descriptive and lacks sustained evaluative depth. This performance best fits the band-2 descriptor for superficial use of data and limited analysis.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question
Clear contrast between revenue gains and profitability delays
weaknesses: Some assertions are made without direct evidence citation
No clear mapping of each conclusion to specific analytical findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion addresses the research question clearly and draws on the investigation, but certain claims lack tight evidence linkage. This corresponds to the band-2 level of partial consistency with evidence.

strengths: Logical overall structure with consistent sub-headings
weaknesses: Some paragraphs are overly long and reduce readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA is appropriately organized throughout, facilitating navigation and coherence. A score of 2 reflects the sound structure with only minor weaknesses in formatting and paragraph length.

strengths: Includes title page, key concept, word count, and references
weaknesses: Typographical and formatting errors in in-text figures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Required elements such as a table of contents are missing and there are noticeable typos, placing the presentation at the band-1 level of one or more required elements missing.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure (introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references) and uses sub-headings consistently, enabling reader navigation.  Minor formatting issues and some fragmented sentences exist, but overall organization is appropriate, warranting the 2-mark band.

Sources are directly relevant (Amazon IR, WSJ financials, Gartner, CNBC) and supply appropriate quantitative data, but depth is uneven: only one academic/industry report and no triangulation for several key figures.  Relevance is clear yet depth and range are merely âsufficient,â aligning with the 3-mark descriptor.

Title page, key concept, word count and references are present, yet required elements such as a complete table of contents and clearly formatted appendices/documents are missing.  Hence, âone or more required elements missingâ applies, awarding 1 mark.
Five supporting documents are cited, satisfying the 3â5 range.  This meets the descriptor for the 3-mark band within the rubricâs gradations for number of documents.

Tools are applied to the research questionâe.g., market-share formula, margin trends tied to diversificationâbut the linkage is sometimes superficial and calculations occasionally contain rounding or labelling issues.  Application is therefore adequate but not consistently clear and effective, meriting the 3-mark band.

Key quantitative data (revenues 2019-23, margins, FCF, market share) are selected, with sources cited.  Some figures lack context (e.g., competitor profitability), but overall selection and basic use are adequate, placing the work in the 3-mark band.

The student analyses trends, links costs of RxPass/Veeqo to margin erosion, and connects market-share gains to diversification; however, evaluation is largely descriptive, integration of tools is sporadic, and counter-argument is thin.  This meets âsome analysis of the research question,â matching the 3-mark descriptor.

The conclusion explicitly answers the research question, stating that diversification increased revenue while delaying profitability.  The response is clear and focused, fulfilling the top descriptor for addressing the research question.

The student selects the Ansoff Matrix, market-share calculation, and profitability ratiosâan adequate toolkit covering both strategic and financial perspectives.  While appropriate, the breadth is not especially wide (e.g., no SWOT, Porter, break-even) so the selection is âadequateâ rather than âeffective.â

Conclusions restate major findings and are mostly supported by cited data, yet certain quantitative claims (e.g., ârecovery in 2023 suggests ventures are becoming cash-flow positivesâ) are asserted without full evidence.  This partial alignment with evidence fits the 2-mark band.

The investigation is tightly anchored to Amazon: revenue, operating income, FCF, market-share figures and specific initiatives (RxPass, Veeqo, Buy with Prime).  Discussion continuously relates findings back to how these moves affected Amazonâs performance, demonstrating strongâthough not flawlessâintegration throughout the assessment.  Minor gaps (e.g., limited isolation of venture-specific results) keep it from the âfully integratedâ standard, so the strand fits the 4-mark level.

The student defines the key concept of change, links it to diversification and repeatedly references âchangeâ when discussing Amazonâs strategic pivots.  However, the analysis seldom moves beyond naming the concept; the financial and market discussions are not systematically framed through a change lens (e.g., Lewin, Kotter, drivingârestraining forces).  This shows a good, but not yet strong, conceptual grasp with clear but only occasional analytical use, matching the band for âgood understanding with clear analytical thinking.â

annotations.comment: The inclusion of labor indicators is intriguingâexplain explicitly how workforce changes link to diversification outcomes.
The research question is precisely stated and segmented effectively across two lines, facilitating clear reader understanding of the IAâs focus.
The title page lacks a table of contents, which is a required IB IA element and would improve navigation through the assessment.
The introductionâs background integrates Amazon-specific examples such as Whole Foods and Alexa, anchoring the key concept of change to the organization.
The definition of âchangeâ is overly simplisticâconsider integrating a formal change management model (e.g., Lewinâs three-step) to deepen conceptual analysis.
The âBackground Informationâ section would benefit from clearer linkage between globalisation and diversification to more cohesively frame the research rationale.
The methodology clearly identifies secondary data sources and key analytical frameworks, demonstrating an adequate selection of tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Methodology lacks detail on how conflicting industry articles were triangulated; clarify the validation process to strengthen data reliability.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The third analytical tool description ends abruptly; complete the sentence to fully outline the research approach.
The introductory analysis on page 4 succinctly outlines market share methodology but could frame the numbers in a change-management context.
The market-share formula is applied correctly with clear steps, demonstrating appropriate use of quantitative tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Typographical error âComparyâs Salesâ appears in your formulaâproofread all inline math for clarity and precision.
Consider adding a small table comparing pre- and post-COVID market shares to provide historical context alongside the 2023 figure.
Market data sources for U.S. figures appear to mix Q2 and annual results; clarify time frames to ensure relevance and depth.
The comparative discussion of Shopify and Walmart enriches the organizational analysis and demonstrates strong connection to Amazonâs context.
The lengthy paragraph on competitive landscape hinders readability; break it into focused sub-sections or bullet points.
Introduce a small chart or table to convey multi-year market-share trends visually for clearer analysis.
The narrative link between diversification and competitive entrenchment effectively integrates the concept of change into the analysis.
Financial figures display inconsistent formatting (missing $ signs, margin decimals); standardize presentation for clarity.
The financial analysis section contains abrupt sentence breaks and missing figuresârevise to complete data points and ensure coherence.
The Ansoff Matrix use demonstrates effective selection of a strategic framework to analyze diversification.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Analysis omits discussion of long-term risk differences between market expansion and diversification; explore implications further.
Consider defining quantitative thresholds to distinguish between Ansoff categories based on performance outcomes.
The reference to â-9.1Bâ in the Ansoff discussion is unclearâspecify whether this denotes cost, FCF loss, or another metric.
The conclusion explicitly contrasts revenue gains with profit delays, directly answering the research question with evidence.
Some concluding statements reference recovery figures without linking back to specific data pointsâcite source context for each claim.
To strengthen consistency, map each conclusion assertion to the corresponding analysis tool (market share, ratios, Ansoff).
The final discussion on page 9 repeats prior points verbatim; condense to improve readability and focus.
Consider adding a brief limitations section to acknowledge data constraints and scope boundaries.
How might emerging healthcare regulations alter Amazonâs long-term diversification outlook? Encourage reflection on future risks.
References employ a consistent citation style and include diverse sources, demonstrating adequate document support.
Some web sources lack retrieval dates or DOIs, reducing academic rigor; include complete reference details for consistency.
